thread.content: [Product Manager] Let's build a real-time feed

assumptions.FE: Needs WebSockets or Long-Polling to handle 'real-time' updates without freezing the React thread.
assumptions.BE: Must implement strict rate-limiting on the API; querying on every page refresh will crash the server.
assumptions.DS: Browsing habits are unstructured text. The model needs an async queue because inference takes 3-5 seconds.

thread.content: [Product Manager] Let's build a real-time feed

assumptions.FE: Needs WebSockets or Long-Polling to handle 'real-time' updates without freezing the React thread.
assumptions.BE: Must implement strict rate-limiting on the API; querying on every page refresh will crash the server.
assumptions.DS: Browsing habits are unstructured text. The model needs an async queue because inference takes 3-5 seconds.
assumptions.blockers: Backend and Frontend are assuming different definitions of 'real-time'. Data Science inference latency (3-5s) conflicts with Frontend expectation of instant updates. No agreement on WebSocket vs. Polling strategy.

dictionary.Real-time: AMBIGUOUS — Frontend assumes <100ms updates; Backend assumes 'near real-time' (~5s tolerance).
dictionary.Rate-limiting: Restricting API requests per client to prevent server overload; must define: requests/second threshold, burst allowance, and 429 response behavior.
dictionary.Async Queue: Decoupled task processing system (e.g., Celery, RabbitMQ) where requests are queued and workers process them independently of the HTTP request/response cycle.
